hero.headline: Brenda Bispo // Backend Software Developer
hero.subheadline: Helping build resilient, scalable systems that impact millions of users daily.
hero.highlight: Part of the engineering team at Mercado Livre, working on Full Super — Mercado Livre's grocery delivery — where I face the challenges of a distributed ecosystem across Latin America. I'm passionate about my craft because I know that what we build impacts real people's everyday lives.
hero.intro: Rooted in Itagimirim, a small town in Bahia, Brazil, but my code reaches all of Latin America. Backend-focused with Java, but never afraid of new languages — I also code in Kotlin, Go, JavaScript, and TypeScript. I develop high-performance microservices while collaborating daily with people from diverse cultures in Argentina, Colombia, Uruguay, and Chile.

journey.title: Developer Journey

levels.title: Roots
levels.description: Born and raised in Itagimirim, Bahia — a small town that forged my determination and relentless curiosity to go beyond.

levels.title: Federal Institute of Bahia
levels.description: Completed high school at IFBA, building a strong academic foundation and sparking my curiosity for science and technology.

levels.title: University & Health Sciences
levels.description: Moved to a new city to study Health Sciences and Biomedicine at UFSB. A stage of personal and intellectual growth.

levels.title: First Tech Project
levels.description: At university, I created "Patógenos Bioecológicos" — an educational game about biological pathogens using block programming that generated Kotlin under the hood. My first encounter with code.

levels.title: Transition to Technology
levels.description: The passion for solving problems through code spoke louder. I decided to switch to software engineering and never looked back.

levels.title: Mercado Livre Bootcamp
levels.description: Entered the Mercado Livre ecosystem through the bootcamp, diving headfirst into software engineering at scale. The start of my professional career.

levels.title: Backend Engineer
levels.description: Became a Backend Software Developer, deepening my knowledge of distributed systems, microservices, and the Meli ecosystem.

levels.title: Supermarket Team
levels.description: Working on the Supermarket team at Mercado Livre — understanding the business, participating in on-call rotations, and evolving the platform daily.

levels.title: Purpose-Driven Projects
levels.description: Building apps that go beyond code: AntiBet Alerta, to fight gambling addiction; Liv+, to help patients with chronic rheumatological diseases manage their daily treatment — and more to come.

levels.title: Next Chapter
levels.description: The journey continues. New technologies to master, new systems to architect, new challenges to overcome. The best is yet to come.

journey.startSubtitle: An adventure through my career path
journey.startHint: Use ← → to move, ↑ to jump, ↓ to drop

experience.title: Backend Engineer – Mercado Livre (3+ years)
experience.intro: Helping build resilient, scalable systems that impact millions of users daily.

achievements.title: Reliability at Scale
achievements.description: On-call rotations ensuring the health of critical user flows in production.

achievements.title: Performance Engineering
achievements.description: Built observability dashboards and metrics for high-traffic APIs (Cart and Checkout).

achievements.title: Mentorship & Quality
achievements.description: Over 40 PR Reviews focused on security, performance, and maintainability.

achievements.title: Safe Rollouts
achievements.description: Feature flag activations, A/B experiments, and regression testing for zero-impact deployments.

achievements.title: Service Migrations
achievements.description: Legacy service migrations and automated validation scripts.

achievements.title: Multicultural Collaboration
achievements.description: Daily collaboration with engineers from Argentina, Colombia, Uruguay, and Chile in a distributed environment.

projects.title: Personal Lab
projects.intro: Beyond the scale of work, I use personal projects to explore new technologies and solve real everyday problems.

project1.title: Patógenos Bioecológicos
project1.description: A crossover between education and technology.
project1.goal: Teaching biological pathogen concepts through interactive gameplay.
project1.tech: Built with Kotlin and block-based programming logic.

project2.title: AntiBet Alerta
project2.description: Android app focused on social impact.
project2.purpose: Detecting gambling websites and sending alerts to help fight addiction.

project3.title: Liv+
project3.purpose: Helping patients with chronic rheumatological diseases manage their daily treatment, and in the future enabling doctors to track patient progress.
